title: Basic CTE
instruction:
A CTE (WITH clause) names a subquery so the main query can reference it. This makes complex queries much more readable.

Run this.
successMessage: The CTE filters to completed orders first, then the main query aggregates. This is cleaner than a nested subquery and easier to debug.

title: Multiple CTEs
instruction:
Chain multiple CTEs to build up logic in stages — each CTE can reference previous ones.

Run this.
successMessage: Two CTEs: `rep_totals` aggregates, `top_reps` filters. Each stage is named and readable. Try rewriting this as nested subqueries — it's much harder to follow.

title: Recursive CTE — number sequence
instruction:
Recursive CTEs reference themselves to build sequences or traverse trees. The base case + recursive step pattern works like a loop.

Run this.
starter:
WITH RECURSIVE numbers(n) AS (
  SELECT 1            -- base case
  UNION ALL
  SELECT n + 1        -- recursive step
  FROM numbers
  WHERE n < 5         -- stop condition
)
SELECT n FROM numbers;
successMessage: The recursive CTE starts at 1 and adds 1 each iteration until n reaches 5. This pattern is used for date ranges, org chart traversals, and path finding.
